articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

article.headline: Gallardo, Funes Mori score as Mexico roll past Iraq in pre-World Cup friendly
article.description: Goals from Alexis Vega, Rogelio Funes Mori, Jesus Gallardo and Uriel Antuna led Mexico to a 4-0 friendly victory against Iraq.
article.linkText: Gallardo, Antuna score as Mexico thrash Iraq

article.story:
Goals from Alexis Vega, Rogelio Funes Mori, Jesus Gallardo and Uriel Antuna led Mexico to a 4-0 victory against Iraq during a friendly at Girona, Spain's Estadi Montilivi.

The Wednesday night exhibition match, which is the second-to-last for Mexico before the World Cup, began on a bright note for the CONCACAF nation that found the back of the net in the fourth minute. Following a pacey run from Gallardo at left-back, the defender sent over a cross to Vega, who finished the move.

- World Cup Kits: Ranking every teams' jerseys in Qatar

After a quiet end to the first half that featured a highly defensive approach from Iraq, a handful of substitutions during the midway break provided El Tri with solutions in the final third.

Halftime additions Antuna and Funes Mori made an immediate impact once they stepped onto the pitch, with Antuna providing a perfect pass from the right flank that Funes Mori finished from close-range to make it 2-0 by the 48th minute.

Then, in the 67th, Gallardo pounced on a poor clearance from Iraq and sent a shot into the back of the net, pushing El Tri up to 3-0. Moments before the final whistle, Funes Mori won a penalty in injury time that set up Antuna to score from the spot in the 92nd minute to seal the 4-0 result.

Manager Gerardo "Tata" Martino and his Mexico squad will continue their World Cup preparation with one last friendly against Sweden, which is also set to be held at the Estadi Montilivi. In Qatar, they'll face Poland (Nov. 22), Saudi Arabia (Nov. 26), and Argentina (Nov. 30) in the group stage.

Martino is expected to release his final World Cup roster on Monday, Nov. 16. One of the Mexico coach's biggest decisions will be whether or not to include injured striker Raul Jimenez, who is in the provisional squad but did not play on Wednesday. Backup players like Erick Sanchez and Jesus Angulo are also on the fringe for inclusion in Martino's final 26-man roster.

Iraq failed to qualify for the World Cup.
